properties.remark: Webcams and Highway Observations confirm 1/8 mile visibility with frequent wind gusts 50+ MPH along I-80 between MM 261-267.

Numerous Webcams and Highway Observations along I-80 between MMs 260 and 275 confirm visibilities to 1/8 mile or less with wind gusts 55+ MPH. The WYDOT sensor at Wagonhound recorded a visibility of 0.05 mile with a 63 MPH wind gust at 256 PM.
The WYDOT sensor at Arlington (KARL) recorded a visibility of 1/10 mile and a peak wind gust to 51 MPH.
Webcams and WYDOT observations confirm visibilities below 1/8th of a mile in heavy snow and strong winds up to 50 MPH for over 30 minutes.
Arlington east WYDOT sensor reported wind gusts of 40 MPH and heavy snow with visibility down to below one quarter of a mile for 50 minutes.
WYDOT sensor at Sinclair and ASOS at Rawlins both reported less than one quarter mile visibility and wind gusts up to 47 MPH.
WYDOT OBSERVATION AT STROUSS HILL reported 1/8th of a mile visibility and wind gusts of 50 MPH for 15 minutes.

ASOS station KRWL 1 ENE Rawlins reported one-quarter mile visibility in falling and blowing snow along with winds gusting up to 45 mph for the past 8 minutes. Visibility has been below one-half mile with similar conditions for the past 30 minutes.
ASOS station KLAR Laramie Airport - Lar reported one-quarter mile visibility in blowing and falling snow with a snow squall along with gusts up to 37 mph for the past 7 minutes.

Mesonet station KVDW Vedauwoo (WYDOT) along Interstate 80 reported below one-quarter mile visibility in falling and blowing snow with a snow squall along with winds gusting 55 mph for the past 14 minutes.
Mesonet station KVDW Vedauwoo (WYDOT) reported one-quarter mile visibility in falling and blowing snow with a snow squall along with wind gusting up to 37 mph for the past 28 minutes.
Mesonet station WY36 2.4 NW Buford (WYDOT) reported one-quarter mile visibility in falling and blowing snow with a snow squall along with wind gusting up to 34 mph for the past 14 minutes.
Mesonet station WY28 3.9 NW Vedauwoo (WYDOT) reported one-quarter mile visibility in falling and blowing snow with a snow squall along with wind gusting up to 33 mph for the past 26 minutes.
Numerous reports of a funnel cloud with occasional ground circulation between Huntley and Lyman. Separate event from earlier landspout.

Flash flooding reported by fire department member. All draws around Gurley are flooded and over banks. Still rain heavily. Fire department starting to flag roads that are flooded.
Report of 3.25 inches of rain and still raining hard. Area experiencing flash flooding currently.

Several photos of water flowing over county roads with significant water build up along the sides of the roads.
Images of water rapidly flowing over the road. Based on images, a small portion of the road may have been damaged.

Report from Emergency Manager. Flooded field.
Road 119 southeast of Dalton has water still flowing over road and appears washed out in picture.
Many reports from Emergency Manageers of flooding along Road 50. Nearly every quarter to half mile is flooded with water flowing over the road.
Received a video from an Emergency Manager of water flowing over Road 117 north of Road 50. Road appears to be washed out.

NWS Storm Survey found EF-1 Tornado damage along an approximately 2 mile long path between Morrill and Mitchell. Several homes sustained roof damage with partial roof removal on one home. A complete industrial garage collapse occurred along US 26 near the end of the damage path.
Trees down on south Mitchell road

KNEB Station Manager reports one of the stations broadcast towers was blown down and collapsed. 300 foot tower. Estimating 750 plus trees down in Scottsbluff and Gering.
Damage reported includes a leaning power pole, trees down, which were snapped at the base (estimated 12 inches near base of tree).

From News Director for western Scotts Bluff County: A lot of damage out on the western side of the county, large trees completely uprooted a number of pivots turned over and at least one grain bin Ive seen damaged. A good number of County gravel roads are flooded as well and I talked to the roads Department theyre closing things off as quick as they can. Western Scotts Bluff County.
